principle: Human-only immersive runtime. Users may bring agent-powered tools/apps, but they cannot create or spawn autonomous agents inside the runtime.

modes.name: Discussion Room
modes.description: A shared space for human participants to discuss a topic, capture decisions, and export notes.

modes.name: Role Play
modes.description: A human-led simulation where participants act through assigned roles and situations.

modes.name: Virtual Playground
modes.description: A sandbox space for exploring concepts, layouts, campaigns, and content experiences.

modes.name: AR Overlay
modes.description: An augmented reality layer for mapping content, notes, objects, or flows onto the real world.

modes.name: VR World
modes.description: An immersive virtual world for workshops, planning, demos, rehearsals, and experience testing.

toolApps.name: Writing Assistant Tool
toolApps.capability: Suggests outlines, rewrites, titles, summaries, and SEO metadata on request.

toolApps.name: Visual Composer Tool
toolApps.capability: Creates canvas-ready layouts, image prompts, and visual asset briefs.

toolApps.name: Research Board Tool
toolApps.capability: Organizes notes, claims, citations, and discussion takeaways.

toolApps.name: Moderator Timer Tool
toolApps.capability: Keeps sessions structured with agenda, timer, and turn prompts.

toolApps.name: Artifact Exporter Tool
toolApps.capability: Exports created notes, decisions, scripts, canvases, and plans to real-world content formats.

runtimeRestrictions: No autonomous agent creation inside the immersive runtime.
Agent-like capabilities are exposed only as user-invoked tools or apps.
Human participants control the session, roles, artifacts, and publishing decisions.
Artifacts must be exportable to real-world files or publishing workflows.
